etymology.description: Depicts a kneeling figure turning around with mouth open -> ancient form ă’«
etymology.interpretation: The character æ—¡(jĂ¬, "partial phonetic") represents a figure that is kneeling and turning around with an open mouth, suggesting a posture or action of abstaining or being in a state of emptiness. It visually contrasts with æ¬ (æ¬ ) (qiĂ n, "owe"), which depicts an open-mouthed figure in a front-facing position. The depiction implies an action or state of having finished or abstaining, closely linked with this pictographic form's original function in the related compound æ—¢(æ—¢) (jĂ¬, "already").
